characters.description: XYwas mostly part of the series title, due to it being the genetic notation for a male. Its only known appearance is on the tank 781227 was created in, and is spoken only twice. "781227 becomesKylein thePilotepisode, as a foster child ofNicoleandStephenTrager, and is legally adopted in Season 2. 781227was the code name for an experiment byAdam Baylinin prolonging human gestation. Adam himself had been the only successful test of that theory in earlier investigations. He had been created as an arranged embryo with thirteen months in a surrogate mother's womb, and consequently had extraordinary mental abilities. The hope was to create an advanced civilization. There is no definitive information about the embryo used for 781227. There are two photos of Adam at the age of Kyle so that they are identical. However, there isno mention by Adam or others of cloning. There is a dubious claim, in the final moment of the final episode, that Kyle sharesGrace Kingsleyas genetic mother withMichael Cassidy.

He had been created as an arranged embryo with thirteen months in a surrogate mother's womb, and consequently had extraordinary mental abilities. The hope was to create an advanced civilization. There is no definitive information about the embryo used for 781227. There are two photos of Adam at the age of Kyle so that they are identical. However, there isno mention by Adam or others of cloning. There is a dubious claim, in the final moment of the final episode, that Kyle sharesGrace Kingsleyas genetic mother withMichael Cassidy.
The character that we will know asJessibegins life as781228 XX.

characters.name: Allison
characters.description: AllisonBiographical InformationStatusAlivePhysical InformationGenderFemaleAffiliationsOccupationsReceptionistAppearancesPortrayed BySonya SalomaaFirst episodeThe Lies That BindLast episodeThe Lies That BindAppears InSeason 1 Allisonis a temporary receptionist on her first at the office whereStephen Tragerworks. The phone calls are many and complaining, because the company computers are down. Stephen has to tell her thatBradis the CEO, and that he is the VP. To take his turn tending toKyle, Stephen takes him to his office. Stephen is in a hurry to have an important private meeting with Brad, so he leaves Kyle with Allison. She is not able to pay any attention to him, because the day is too hectic. Kyle talks with one of Stephen's staff, then wanders into the little room where the computer server is. When Stephen comes out of the meeting, Allison tells him that Kyle is gone, but has been gone only for a few minutes. They discover him on the floor with the server in pieces. She and Stephen rush to the main phone station. They then follow Brad, who is upset because his email is not working, on the way to the computer room to see what the problem is. Allison is last seen looking in the server room behind Stephen and Brad.

characters.name: Julian Ballantine
characters.description: Julian Ballantineis a African-American executive ofMadacorp. His main project is theCIR, which he intends to use to control a person's mind with upload and download and delete like a computer. He is the main antagonist for the first half of season 2. Ballantine inherited hisLatnok ringfrom his father, though it's importance was never revealed to him.

characters.name: Adam Baylin
characters.description: Adam BaylinBiographical InformationFull NameAdam BaylinStatusDeadRelativesParentsUnnamed surrogate mother †Significant OthersSarah Emerson(college girlfriend)ChildrenKyle Trager(genetic son)AppearancesPortrayed ByJ. Eddie Peck(middle age)Matt Dallas(young)First episodeEndgameLast episodeI've Had the Time of My LifeAppears InSeason 1,Season 2 Adam Baylinwas the only living result of a scientific experiment to prolong gestation to increase brain development. He was said to be the most brilliant student in the Science department at the University of Washington. He and his professor,William Kern, founded a secret organization calledZzyzxto continue the experiments using artificial wombs. Zzyzx was financed by a secret society calledLatnokthat Adam was part of. Adam went into seclusion for many years, continuing to develop his mind and special abilities. We learn of Adam from before the story in two main ways: Kyle's developing memory, and Adam instructing Kyle in season 2.

characters.name: Amanda Bloom
characters.description: Amanda BloomBiographical InformationFull NameAmanda BloomStatusAlivePhysical InformationAbilitiesPlaying pianoGenderFemaleHair ColorBlondRelativesParentsUnnamed father †Carol BloomAffiliationsFriendsLori TragerJosh TragerDeclan McDonoughHillary(via Lori)Love InterestsCharlie Tanner(Season 1)Kyle TragerInterestsMusicOccupationsLifeguardStudentAppearancesPortrayed ByKirsten ProutAppears InAll seasonsType of RoleMain Character Amanda Bloomlives next door to the Tragers, and Kyle falls in love with her in the first episode. Their developing romance is of major interest to many viewers, referring to the hoped-for couple as "Kylanda". Kyle first sees Amanda by entering her house to discover the source of her piano playing. Amanda hears and suddenly notices him. Startled, Kyle runs back to the Trager residence. Amanda's mother,Carol, takes her to the Tragers' to complain toNicolethat Kyle had entered their house and frightened her daughter. Kyle is standing there with Nicole, and he begins to fall in love with Amanda. Amanda clearly is not as upset as her mother, and says goodbye when she is dragged away from him. A few nights later, Kyle calms a stray dog by simply staring at it. Amanda, looking out the window, is impressed. Amanda then explains about "down time" and says his family must be searching for him.

characters.name: Brad
characters.description: Bradis the C.E.O. ofLWK SoftwarewhereStephen Trageris a vice president as the head of his own software development team. Stephen arrives late for an important private meeting. Brad tells him that poor earnings mean that some of Stephen's people must be let go to cut costs. Stephen wants to tell them, especially becauseMark Crenshawis about to buy a bigger house. Brad says not to, because it must be a secret from the stockholders.

characters.name: Det. Breen
characters.description: Detective BreenBiographical InformationFull NameJason BreenStatusAliveAffiliationsOrganizationsSeattle policeAppearancesPortrayed ByKurt Max RunteFirst episodeSleepless in SeattleLast episodeOverheardAppears InSeason 1 Jason Breenis a detective assigned to Kyle as a Missing Person, and then in charge of theWilliam Kernmurder case. Detective Breen visits the Trager house to interview Kyle. Kyle can not answer any of his questions, and Det. Breen tellsNicolethat he has never seen an amnesia case like Kyle.

characters.name: Dennis Bunker
characters.description: Dennis BunkerBiographical InformationFull NameDennis BunkerDiedIn S01E04, killed byTom FossStatusDeceasedPhysical InformationSpeciesHumanGenderMaleAffiliationsOrganizationsZzyzxAppearancesPortrayed ByMackenzie GrayAppears InSeason 1Diving In(only) Dennis Bunkeris Personnel Director for "the company" which we know later asZzyzx. Dennis takes the resignation byTom Foss. Dennis says "Ten years of service to the company, Tom.  Sorry to see you go." He makes sure Foss has supplied a forwarding address, saying "The company likes to keep in touch, especially now withKernstill MIA." Bunker reminds Tom of "how aggressively we enforce these confidentiality agreements." Before Foss leaves, Dennis asks for his security card, which Foss seems to have let slip his mind.  It is like the card found with the skeleton in the woods, showing the symbol that Kyle has included in pieces on his drawings.

characters.name: The Camper
characters.description: The first person that781228 XXencounters is a lone and large hunter camping near the hiddenZzyzxcompound. The hunter is seen emerging from a tent to taste from birds roasting on a stick over his campfire, then throwing his big knife at a tree. When "XX" approaches the fire to try to eat as she had seen him do, he is getting a pint liquor bottle.

characters.description: Michael CassidyBiographical InformationStatusAlivePhysical InformationGenderMaleRelativesParentsGrace Kingsley(mother)SiblingsKyle Trager(half-brother)AffiliationsEnemiesKyleAppearancesPortrayed ByHal OzsanFirst episodeIt Happened One NightLast episodeBringing Down the HouseAppears InSeason 3Type of RoleAntagonist Michael Cassidyis the villain of Season 3.  LikeBallantine, he is very powerful through an organization, and there is something he wants from Kyle.  What he does to get it, is much of Season 3.

characters.name: Dr. Cassidy
characters.description: Dr. Jacob Cassidyis the hypnotist thatNicolebrings in to help Kyle remember something of his past.  While under hypnosis, Kyle says he is in a car and it's raining. He says he is not alone, there is a man looking in,Prof. Kern. Kyle becomes very distressed and says "He's hurting him!" and says that it isFosshurting him. Kyle is so distressed that Nicole tells Dr. Cassidy to get him out of it. Jacob asks Kyle what happened before, and after a pause Kyle suddenly begins to speak an impossibly fast repetetive gibberish.

Jacob asks Kyle what happened before, and after a pause Kyle suddenly begins to speak an impossibly fast repetetive gibberish.

characters.name: Coach
characters.description: The coach for the Bisons is not an entirely nice man, as Kyle discovers on his first day of practice.  After seeing Kyle sink impossible baskets,Charliebrings him to practice, the week before the championship game. Coach says the team is perfectly balanced, and a new player will not be approved by the league. Then, he sees Kyle making baskets in every direction. He is still dubious when Kyle can't make a shot in game practice.

characters.name: Mark Crenshaw
characters.description: Mark Crenshawwas part ofStephen Trager's software development team atLWK Software. There is also theMarkwho datesLori. Stephen takes Kyle to work with him so he wouldn't be left alone at home. Kyle is encountered by Mark in the hallway. He says that Stephen has told him all about him. He tells Kyle that Stephen is the best, and that Stephen's group works as one big family. Mark's cell phone rings and cuts off their conversation. Stephen was there for a meeting withBrad, the CEO, who says that some employees, including Mark, will be let go to save money. Stephen wants to tell them, so they can make plans; but Brad says no, that could make their stock go down.

characters.name: Lou Daniels
characters.description: Lou Danielsis the Director of the Juvenile Detention Center where781227is first taken after being found. When the stange new boy is delivered, Lou callsNicole Trager, knowing that he is her kind of special case as a psychological counselor.  Lou has to save the boy from a fight another boy starts with him. When Nicole arrives, Lou explains that the boy cannot speak and does not know the simplest things about living, but seems very bright.  He also shows her that Kyle doesn't have a belly-button. Nicole names him Kyle when Lou says he is good-looking like his brother, and she takes Kyle home with her for the weekend.

characters.name: L.K. Deichman
characters.description: DeichmanBiographical InformationFull NameL.K. DeichmanAliasGeekman,DykemanStatusAlivePhysical InformationSpeciesHumanGenderMaleAppearancesPortrayed ByCascy BeddowFirst episodeThis is Not a TestLast episodeThis is Not a TestThe List Is Life(mentioned only)Appears InSeason 1 L.K. Deichmanis a student atBeachwood High Schoolwho meets Kyle on his first day there. Unable to answer any of the questions on the tests required for him to enter school,Kylewanders out ofBradford Hooper's office. After visiting the math room, he follows the other students at the next bell to the lunchroom. Kyle is about to sit withAmandawhenCharliedoes. Turning around, he sits next to a boy by himself at table, who is busy drawing in a notebook. The boy says "Oo-oo, I wouldn't sit there," and goes on to explain that he is called Geekman or Dikeman, because his name is Deichman. When Kyle tries to see his comic-book-style drawings, L.K. tries to hide them. When Kyle says he draws too, L.K. explains about the super-heroes who have secret identities "to hide their amazing abilities. So they can help people, without freaking them out."  When L.K. makes a noise with the straw at the bottom of his drink cup, Kyle tries making the same noise.

characters.description: Dougis a school friend ofJosh Tragerin Season 1, along withRobbie. On the first day back in school, looking for classes, Doug and Robbie laugh at Josh when they see that he hasSchultzfor reading, because that class is Remedial.

characters.description: Sarah EmersonBiographical InformationStatusPresumed DeadPhysical InformationGenderFemaleHair ColorBrownEye colorBrownRelativesChildrenJessi TaylorAffiliationsLove InterestsAdam Baylin(ex boyfriend)AppearancesPortrayed ByAlly SheedyFirst episodeGrey MattersLast episodeTell-Tale Heart  Sarah EmersonandAdam Baylinbegan dating sometime before1982, and frequentedJP's Diner. Adam etched their initials into a table during their last trip to the diner. The appearance ofJessias identical to Sarah, and of Kyle as identical to Adam, makes the old man still working in the diner think that "the lovebirds have returned."

characters.name: Eve
characters.description: Eveis a student at theUniversity of Washington. Kyle joinsLoriandDeclanon a trip to the University in a quest to uncover more of his past. While Lori is thinking that "there really is life after high school," Declan is cornered by Eve. She encourages him to come to UW by pointing that UW has her to offer.

characters.name: Tom Foss
characters.description: Tom FossBiographical InformationFull NameTom FossStatusAlivePhysical InformationGenderMaleRelativesSignificant OthersWife †ChildrenDaughter †AffiliationsFriendsAdam Baylin†EnemiesZzyzxMadacorpGoalProtectKyleat all costs,Help Kyle reach his full potentialOccupationsSecurity patrol(formerly)AppearancesPortrayed ByNicholas LeaFirst episodePilotLast episodeGuess Who's Coming to DinnerAppears InAll seasonsType of RoleMain Character Tom Fossis a mysterious character whose agenda is up for grabs for most of Season 1, until we learn that he is Kyle's guardian and protector and a trusted friend of Adam Baylin. Foss is outwardly cold and strict, but he genuinely cares for Kyle and risks his life to protect him. Foss was trained in the military, and his tactics are often brutal. Foss was trained for rescue and torture in the military. This experience left him an alcoholic. One day, he drinks and drives, causing an accident, killing his wife and daughter. After this he starts working for Zzyzx where he bulids an emotional bond with781227, playing him classical music and treating him like a person rather than just a machine. For 10 years he works undercover as security at Zzyzx for Adam, protecting the"ghost in the machine".[1]

After this he starts working for Zzyzx where he bulids an emotional bond with781227, playing him classical music and treating him like a person rather than just a machine. For 10 years he works undercover as security at Zzyzx for Adam, protecting the"ghost in the machine".[1]

characters.name: Nate Harrison
characters.description: Nateis one of the students working in specialLatnokfacilities at theUniversity of Washington, under the direction ofMichael Cassidy.  When Cassidy is first showing Kyle around the student Latnok facility, Nate approaches and says "So the great mind has finally come to play with us mortals." Cassidy says "Nate considers himself top dog" and Nate tells Kyle that he graduated high school at thirteen. When Kyle begins work on theHoverboardcompetition, his workbench is next to Nate's. Nate is offended that Kyle is expecting to design a Hoverboard in two days, which Nate has been working on for a long time, and doesn't understand how Kyle can build the circuit boards. Nate overhears mention ofAmanda, accuses Kyle of stealing his materials, and says he won't help Kyle.

characters.description: Emily Hollanderworks at Madacorp directly under Julian Ballantine as a special agent assigned originally to retrieve 781228, the other experiment at Zzyzx, and then to act as Jessi's sister in a plot against Kyle.

characters.name: Nancy Hollander
characters.description: Nancy Hollanderis the mother ofEmily Hollanderand grandmother toPaige Hollander. While Emily is working undercover forMadacorp, she has to stay at an apartment withJessi, meaning that her daughter Paige has to stay with Nancy. Emily is shocked to hear that a man from Madacorp picked up her daughter from school, and she races to Nancy's home. Paige is safely there, and Nancy tells Emily that the "nice young man from Madacorp" who brought Paige home wanted Emily to know that "they always have her family in mind." Emily gets the threat of that, and follows orders fromBallantinefrom then on.
